findings.message: N+1 query pattern: the endpoint first fetches all item IDs with one query, then issues a separate `session.get(Item, iid)` for every ID in a loop. For a table with N rows this executes N+1 queries. Unlike `GET /items` there is no pagination limit, so the query count is unbounded and grows linearly with table size.
findings.suggestion: Replace the two-step fetch with a single query that selects only the name column: `return list(session.scalars(select(Item.name)))`. This returns all names in one round-trip and avoids loading the full ORM object for each row.
